M-044.NAME: kpproton-runtime-bridge
M-044.path: backend/app/mtproto backend/app/tasks/scheduler.py backend/app/core/config.py mtproto-runtime docs/plans/Phase-30.xml docs/plans/Phase-37.xml docs/plans/Phase-38.xml docs/plans/Phase-41.xml
M-044.owns: Runtime bridge from KrotPN assignments to the KPprotoN/mtproto_proxy policy layer, startup policy replay, and explicit policy revoke.
M-044.purpose: Keep Telegram MTProto access aligned with issued assignment state by loading or removing SNI assignments at the runtime edge without blocking normal KrotPN auth.

exports.fn-apply-domain-policy: Apply one issued SNI to the MTProto policy runtime.
exports.fn-revoke-domain-policy: Remove one issued SNI from the MTProto policy runtime after an explicit admin revoke.
exports.fn-replay-active-assignments: Load active assignments after process or container restart.
exports.fn-runtime-health: Expose bridge health for user-safe and admin-safe status checks.
exports.fn-http-policy-adapter: Apply, revoke, and health-check issued SNI policies through the token-protected MTProto edge policy API, including the planned private DE runtime target.
exports.task-mtproto-policy-sync: Scheduled reconciliation between Postgres assignments and edge policy state.

runtime-policy.policy-1: Bridge failures mark MTProto provisioning degraded but do not prevent normal KrotPN login.
runtime-policy.policy-2: Verified registration may complete with a pending proxy status only if user messaging is explicit.
runtime-policy.policy-3: Startup replay must be idempotent and safe after backup restore.
runtime-policy.policy-4: Runtime revoke must be idempotent by SNI and must remove an already-applied policy without requiring the assignment to remain active.
runtime-policy.policy-5: Provisioning must apply the issued SNI to the live edge before returning an active user payload; failed apply returns an explicit degraded/incomplete state instead of a dead active proxy link.
runtime-policy.policy-6: Phase-38 policy apply may target the DE runtime only over a private, token-protected policy API and must remain degraded-safe when unreachable.

constraints.constraint-1: Do not rely on KPprotoN DETS registry as the source of truth.
constraints.constraint-2: Do not expose raw tg://proxy links or secrets in runtime logs.
constraints.constraint-3: Do not rotate MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX or MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT when enabling the live runtime for existing assignments.

phase-41-reactivation.item-1: Phase-41 supersedes the Phase-40 official MTProxy production path and reactivates M-044 as the production SNI policy bridge.
phase-41-reactivation.item-2: Policy apply/revoke/replay must target the DE KPprotoN runtime and must not depend on official MTProxy secret manifests.
phase-41-reactivation.item-3: Adding a new SNI policy must not restart the MTProto data-plane when the runtime supports in-memory policy updates.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: Phase-30 target pytest passed for fake/local adapter policy apply, replay idempotency, degraded mode, runtime health redaction, and scheduler registration.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-14: Phase-33 fix added runtime policy revoke regression coverage for admin assignment disable.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-18: Phase-37 added HTTPMTProtoPolicyAdapter, provisioning-time live policy apply, token-protected Erlang policy handler, and local regression coverage for live edge policy calls.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 plan keeps the same bridge contract but moves the production policy target from RU-local runtime to the private DE runtime.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-18: Phase-38 local execution permits private DE policy URLs, rejects public/wildcard policy API targets, and adds HTTP adapter trace markers for apply/health operations.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-18: DE live smoke found mtproto_proxy bootstrap still hardcoded to 127.0.0.1 while policy API was privately bound to 172.29.255.1; runtime now builds bootstrap URLs from POLICY_LISTEN_IP.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-18: DE live rebuild passed with mtproto_proxy listening on 0.0.0.0:443 and private policy health OK on 172.29.255.1:18080.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-19: Phase-40 planning marks KPprotoN/Erlang data-plane as insufficient after req_pq timeout against issued user links.
evidence.evidence-9: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design reopens the KPprotoN runtime bridge as the intended production policy plane after official MTProxy proved operationally mismatched for per-user issuance.

M-044.hotspot: H-030: MTProto runtime bridge must degrade safely when policy loading fails and must not block normal KrotPN login
